title: Guide
subtitle: Learn how to use LangHire and reference this anytime

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.pipelineDescription: Set up your profile → Configure AI → Collect jobs from LinkedIn → Agent applies automatically → Learns from each run → Gets better over time

checklist.title: Setup Checklist
checklist.allDone: All required steps are complete! You're ready to go.

profile.title: Setting up your profile
profile.intro: Your candidate profile is the core information the AI agent uses when filling out job applications. Go to
profile.essentialDesc: Name, Email, Phone — these appear on every application.
profile.targetTitlesDesc: These are the search terms used when collecting jobs from LinkedIn. Add multiple titles to cast a wider net (e.g., "Data Analyst", "Business Analyst", "Junior Data Scientist").
profile.targetLocationsDesc: Geographic preferences for job search (e.g., "San Francisco, CA", "Remote").
profile.skillsDesc: These help the agent answer skills-related questions on application forms.
profile.notesDesc: Free-text instructions for the AI agent. Use this for special instructions like "Only apply to fully remote positions" or "I prefer companies under 500 employees."

aiProvider.title: Configuring your AI provider
aiProvider.intro: The AI model is the "brain" that drives the browser automation. It reads web pages, decides what to click, fills in form fields, and answers application questions. Go to
aiProvider.recommendedDesc: OpenAI GPT-4o or Anthropic Claude Sonnet — both work well. Cost is typically $0.02–0.10 per application depending on form complexity.
aiProvider.bedrockDesc: If you have AWS credentials, you can use Claude through Bedrock. Supports both AWS CLI profiles and access key/secret key authentication.
aiProvider.testConnectionDesc: Always click "Test Connection" after entering your credentials to verify everything works before running operations.

login.title: Logging into LinkedIn & Gmail
login.introEnd: within its browser window. No separate login step is needed.
login.howItWorksDesc: When you start collecting or applying, the agent opens a browser and checks if you're logged into LinkedIn. If not, it waits for you to log in manually in that same browser window. Once it detects you're logged in (the LinkedIn feed loads), it proceeds automatically.
login.firstTimeDesc: You'll need to log in once when the browser opens. After that, your session is saved in the browser profile and persists across runs.
login.gmailDesc: If an external job site sends a verification code, the agent will open Gmail in a new tab to retrieve the code. You may need to log into Gmail the first time this happens.
login.sessionExpiredDesc: If LinkedIn logs you out (rare), the agent will detect the login page and wait for you to log in again. Watch the live log — it will say "Login required."

collecting.title: Collecting jobs
collecting.intro: Job collection searches LinkedIn for listings matching your target job titles and locations. Go to
collecting.howItWorksDesc: An AI agent opens LinkedIn, searches for each of your target job titles, scrolls through results, and saves every job it finds (title, company, location, URL, Easy Apply status).
collecting.timingDesc: Collection typically takes 3–10 minutes per job title, depending on how many results there are. You can watch the live log to see progress.
collecting.maxJobsDesc: Set a limit (e.g., 20) to stop after a certain number. Leave blank to collect all available results.
collecting.singleTitleDesc: Enter a specific title in the search box, or leave blank to search all titles from your profile.

applying.title: Applying to jobs
applying.intro: Once you've collected jobs, go to
applying.easyApplyDesc: LinkedIn's built-in application form. The agent fills in your profile info, uploads your resume, answers questions, and submits — all within a modal dialog on LinkedIn.
applying.externalSitesDesc: For jobs that redirect to an external ATS (Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, etc.), the agent navigates to the external site, creates an account if needed, and fills out the full application.
applying.tailoredResumesDesc: In "Tailored" mode, the agent first reads the job description, extracts key skills, and creates a customized version of your resume with those skills added before applying.
applying.limitDesc: Set a number to cap how many jobs to apply to in one session. Leave blank for no limit.
applying.blockedDomainsDesc: If certain sites cause issues, add them to the blocked list in
applying.blockedDomainsEnd: . The agent will skip jobs on those domains.

memory.title: Understanding the Memory system
memory.intro: The memory system is what makes the agent get
memory.introEnd: . After each application attempt, the agent extracts procedural learnings about the website and stores them for future use.
memory.howItWorksDesc: Before each job, memories for that website are injected into the agent's prompt. The agent then knows things like "Workday requires creating an account first" or "LinkedIn Easy Apply opens in a modal, don't navigate away."
memory.atsNormalizationDesc: Learnings from one Workday site (e.g., goodyear.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com) automatically help on ALL Workday sites. The system recognizes 20+ ATS platforms.
memory.categoryNavigationDesc: How to find and reach the application form
memory.categoryFormStrategyDesc: Overall approach to completing the form
memory.categoryFailureDesc: How to handle errors and blockers
memory.categorySiteStructureDesc: General layout of the application flow
memory.categoryQADesc: Common question types and input formats
memory.maintenanceDesc: Over time, memories can become stale (websites update their UI). Use "Decay" to reduce confidence of old memories and "Cleanup" to remove low-confidence ones. View and manage all memories on the

tips.title: Tips & Troubleshooting
tips.oneOperation: Only one operation at a time:
tips.oneOperationDesc: The app uses a shared browser profile, so only one browser operation (collect, apply, or login) can run at a time.
tips.nonDeterministicDesc: The AI may succeed or fail on the same job across different runs. If a job fails, it stays in your queue for the next attempt.
tips.rateLimitsDesc: LinkedIn may temporarily block Easy Apply if too many applications are submitted in a short time. Space out your application sessions.
tips.browserStuckDesc: If an operation seems frozen, click "Stop" — this kills the browser process. Then try again.
tips.loginExpiredDesc: If the agent encounters auth errors, go back to the Dashboard and log in again. Sessions occasionally expire.
tips.resumeUploadDesc: Make sure the file path in Settings points to an actual PDF file on your computer.
tips.backendNotConnectingDesc: In development mode, make sure the Python backend is running:
tips.costsDesc: Each application costs ~$0.02–0.10 in LLM API calls depending on form complexity. Monitor your provider's usage dashboard.

setupWizard.title: Need to re-run the setup?
setupWizard.description: Open the Setup Wizard again to walk through configuration step-by-step.
